api/GardaVettingSystem.Models.AccessCode.title: Class AccessCode | Garda Vetting System
api/GardaVettingSystem.Models.AccessCode.summary: Class AccessCode Namespace GardaVettingSystem.Models Assembly GardaVettingSystem.dll Represents a time-limited access code generated by an applicant for a specific organisation. Access codes allow organisations to retrieve an applicant's vetting data without the applicant needing to re-enter their information. Codes are designed to be revocable and time-limited in line with GDPR principles of integrity and confidentiality. public class AccessCode Inheritance object AccessCode Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AccessCodeId Primary key — auto-generated unique identifier for the access code record. [Key] public int AccessCodeId { get; set; } Property Value int Applicant Navigation property — the applicant who generated this access code. public Applicant? Applicant { get; set; } Property Value Applicant ApplicantNumber Foreign key referencing the Applicant who generated this access code. Always set server-side — never accepted from form input. [ForeignKey("Applicant")] public int ApplicantNumber { get; set; } Property Value int Code Gets or sets the generated access code value. Codes are 12-character alphanumeric strings generated using RandomNumberGenerator for cryptographic security. Characters I and O are excluded to avoid visual ambiguity with digits 1 and 0. Always set server-side — never accepted from form input. [Required] [StringLength(20, ErrorMessage = "Code cannot exceed 20 characters")] [Display(Name = "Access Code")] public string Code { get; set; } Property Value string CreatedDate Gets or sets the date and time the access code was created. Stored as DateTimeOffset for locale and timezone independence. Always set server-side to UTC at the time of generation. [Display(Name = "Created Date")] public DateTimeOffset CreatedDate { get; set; } Property Value DateTimeOffset ExpiryDate Gets or sets the date and time the access code expires. Codes expire 30 days after creation by default, supporting GDPR data minimisation principles. A null value indicates no expiry has been set. [Display(Name = "Expiry Date")] public DateTimeOffset? ExpiryDate { get; set; } Property Value DateTimeOffset? IsActive Gets or sets whether this access code is currently active. Set to false when the applicant revokes the code. Revoked codes are retained in the database rather than deleted to preserve the audit trail. [Display(Name = "Active")] public bool IsActive { get; set; } Property Value bool OrganisationName Gets or sets the name of the organisation this access code was generated for. [Required] [StringLength(100, ErrorMessage = "Organisation name cannot exceed 100 characters")] [Display(Name = "Organisation Name")] public string OrganisationName { get; set; } Property Value string

api/GardaVettingSystem.Models.Applicant..title: Class Applicant | Garda Vetting System
api/GardaVettingSystem.Models.Applicant..summary: Class Applicant Namespace GardaVettingSystem.Models Assembly GardaVettingSystem.dll Represents an applicant's personal profile in the Garda Vetting Data Reuse System. Each registered user can have one applicant profile, which stores personal information required for Garda vetting applications. The profile can be shared with organisations via generated access codes, eliminating the need for repeated data entry. public class Applicant Inheritance object Applicant Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AccessCodes Navigation property — the collection of access codes generated by this applicant. Access codes allow organisations to view the applicant's vetting data. public ICollection<AccessCode> AccessCodes { get; } Property Value ICollection<AccessCode> ApplicantAddresses Navigation property — the collection of addresses associated with this applicant. An applicant can have multiple addresses representing their address history. public ICollection<ApplicantAddress> ApplicantAddresses { get; } Property Value ICollection<ApplicantAddress> ApplicantNumber Primary key — auto-generated unique identifier for the applicant profile. [Key] public int ApplicantNumber { get; set; } Property Value int BirthLastName Gets or sets the applicant's surname at birth, if different from their current last name. Optional field. [StringLength(50, ErrorMessage = "Birth surname cannot exceed 50 characters")] [Display(Name = "Surname at birth")] public string BirthLastName { get; set; } Property Value string BirthPlace Gets or sets the applicant's place of birth. Optional field. [StringLength(50, ErrorMessage = "Birth place cannot exceed 50 characters")] [Display(Name = "Place of birth")] public string BirthPlace { get; set; } Property Value string DateOfBirth Gets or sets the applicant's date of birth. Stored as DateTimeOffset rather than DateTime to ensure locale and timezone independence across different operating system regional settings. [Required(ErrorMessage = "Date of Birth is required")] [Display(Name = "Date of birth")] public DateTimeOffset? DateOfBirth { get; set; } Property Value DateTimeOffset? FirstName Gets or sets the applicant's first name. [Required(ErrorMessage = "First name is required")] [StringLength(50, ErrorMessage = "First name cannot exceed 50 characters")] [Display(Name = "First Name")] public string FirstName { get; set; } Property Value string FullName Returns the applicant's full name as a concatenation of first and last name. public string FullName { get; } Property Value string Gender Gets or sets the applicant's gender. [Required(ErrorMessage = "Gender is required")] [Display(Name = "Gender")] public string Gender { get; set; } Property Value string LastName Gets or sets the applicant's last name. [Required(ErrorMessage = "Last name is required")] [StringLength(50, ErrorMessage = "Last name cannot exceed 50 characters")] [Display(Name = "Last Name")] public string LastName { get; set; } Property Value string MothersLastName Gets or sets the applicant's mother's maiden name. Optional field required for some vetting applications. [StringLength(50, ErrorMessage = "Mother's maiden name cannot exceed 50 characters")] [Display(Name = "Mother's maiden name")] public string MothersLastName { get; set; } Property Value string UserId The ASP.NET Identity user ID of the logged-in user who owns this profile. Used to enforce ownership — each user can only access their own profile. public string UserId { get; set; } Property Value string

api/GardaVettingSystem.Models.ApplicantA.title: Class ApplicantAddress | Garda Vetting System
api/GardaVettingSystem.Models.ApplicantA.summary: Class ApplicantAddress Namespace GardaVettingSystem.Models Assembly GardaVettingSystem.dll Represents a single address entry in an applicant's address history. An applicant can have multiple addresses covering different periods of residence. Address history is a standard requirement for Garda vetting applications. public class ApplicantAddress Inheritance object ApplicantAddress Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AddressId Primary key — auto-generated unique identifier for the address record. [Key] public int AddressId { get; set; } Property Value int AddressLine Gets or sets the full address line for this residence. [Required(ErrorMessage = "Address is required")] [StringLength(250, ErrorMessage = "Address cannot exceed 250 characters")] [Display(Name = "Address")] public string AddressLine { get; set; } Property Value string Applicant Navigation property — the applicant this address belongs to. public Applicant? Applicant { get; set; } Property Value Applicant ApplicantNumber Foreign key referencing the Applicant this address belongs to. Always set server-side — never accepted from form input. [ForeignKey("Applicant")] public int ApplicantNumber { get; set; } Property Value int Country Gets or sets the country for this address. Optional field. [StringLength(50, ErrorMessage = "Country cannot exceed 50 characters")] public string Country { get; set; } Property Value string Postcode Gets or sets the postcode for this address. Stored as Postcode rather than Eircode to accommodate applicants who lived abroad, as Eircode is Ireland-specific. Optional field. [StringLength(10, ErrorMessage = "Postcode/Eircode cannot exceed 10 characters")] [Display(Name = "Postcode/Eircode")] public string Postcode { get; set; } Property Value string ResidentFrom Gets or sets the year the applicant began residing at this address. Stored as an integer year rather than a full date — a deliberate design decision as Garda vetting applications require year-level precision only. [Required(ErrorMessage = "Resident from year is required")] [Display(Name = "Resident From")] public int? ResidentFrom { get; set; } Property Value int? ResidentTo Gets or sets the year the applicant stopped residing at this address. A null value indicates this is the applicant's current address. [Display(Name = "Resident To")] public int? ResidentTo { get; set; } Property Value int?

api/GardaVettingSystem.Pages.AccessCodes.summary: Class ValidateModel Namespace GardaVettingSystem.Pages.AccessCodes Assembly GardaVettingSystem.dll Handles validation of an access code entered by an organisation. api/GardaVettingSystem.Pages.Applicants..title: Namespace GardaVettingSystem.Pages.Applicants | Garda Vetting System
api/GardaVettingSystem.Pages.Applicants..summary: Namespace GardaVettingSystem.Pages.Applicants Classes CreateModel Handles the creation of a new applicant profile for the logged-in user. Each user can only have one profile — if one already exists, the user is redirected to Edit instead. DeleteModel Handles the deletion of the logged-in user's applicant profile. Ownership is verified server-side — users can only delete their own profile. After deletion the user is redirected to the application root as they no longer have a profile. DetailsModel Displays the logged-in user's applicant profile, including their address history and access codes. This is the central hub page of the application as documented in the system workflow. EditModel Handles the editing of the logged-in user's applicant profile. Ownership is verified server-side on both GET and POST — users can only edit their own profile. IndexModel Acts as a smart redirect entry point for the Applicants section. Routes the logged-in user directly to their profile Details page, or to Create if no profile exists. Also handles post-deletion confirmation and full account deletion including the Identity user record.

Clone the Repository git clone https://github.com/annmariedunne/garda-vetting-system.git 2. Open the Project Open GardaVettingSystem.sln in Visual Studio. 3. Check the Connection String In appsettings.json confirm the connection string is correct: "ConnectionStrings": { "GardaVettingSystemDbContextConnection": "Server=(localdb)\\mssqllocaldb;Database=GardaVettingSystem;Trusted_Connection=True;MultipleActiveResultSets=true" } 4. Create the Database Open Tools → NuGet Package Manager → Package Manager Console and run: Update-Database This creates the database and tables. 5. Run the Application Press F5 in Visual Studio to compile and run the build or run this in the console: dotnet run Features Volunteer registration and secure authentication via ASP.NET Identity Single applicant profile per registered user Full address history management Cryptographically secure 12-character access code generation Organisation-side code validation — no login required Access code revocation with audit trail preserved PDF export of vetting profile GDPR-compliant — full right to view, edit and delete personal data Project Structure GardaVettingSystem/ ├── Data/ │ ├── GardaVettingSystemDbContext.cs # Database context │ └── Migrations/ # EF Core migrations ├── Models/ │ ├── Applicant.cs # Applicant profile model │ ├── ApplicantAddress.cs # Address history model │ └── AccessCode.cs # Access code model ├── Pages/ │ ├── Applicants/ # Applicant profile CRUD pages │ ├── ApplicantAddresses/ # Address history CRUD pages │ ├── AccessCodes/ # Access code CRUD pages │ └── Shared/ # Layout and partials ├── Services/ │ └── VettingPdfService.cs # PDF export service └── wwwroot/ # Static assets Running the Tests Open Package Manager Console and run: dotnet test

index.html.title: Garda Vetting System | Garda Vetting System
index.html.summary: Garda Vetting System A secure web-based data reuse system for Garda vetting applications, developed as a Final Year Project at Atlantic Technological University. Overview The Garda Vetting System allows Irish volunteers to store their personal vetting information once and share it with multiple organisations via a generated access code, reducing the administrative burden of repeated vetting applications. Key Features Volunteer registration and secure authentication Personal vetting profile — store and manage personal data in one place Address history — full address history required for Garda vetting Access code generation — cryptographically secure 12-character codes Organisation validation — organisations can validate codes without logging in PDF export — download vetting profile as a PDF document GDPR compliance — full right to view, edit and delete personal data Technology Used ASP.NET Core Razor Pages (.NET 10) Entity Framework Core with SQL Server LocalDB ASP.NET Identity NUnit GitHub Actions (CI/CD) CodeQL and SonarQube DocFX for documentation Getting Started See the Getting Started for build and run instructions. readme.html.title: Generating docs with DocFx | Garda Vetting System
readme.html.summary: Generating docs with DocFx DocFX Documentation Open terminal in Visual Studio which is the Developer Powershell - View | Terminal. Change to the docs folder at the repository root. cd docs Run this command to install docfx on the machine if not already installed. This will install or update docfx. dotnet tool update -g docfx Run this command to generate the Help files: docfx docfx.json --serve This will generate the documentation You should be able to use the browser to see the docs at http://localhost:8080 The docs themselves are a static website in the docs/_site folder. To view the docs without regenerating them run docfx serve _site If you have any issues viewing the localhost url try: http://127.0.0.1:8080/
